LAW AND MOTION 302, JUL-19-2023. HEARING RE: DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. THE COURT CONFIRMS THAT NOTICE TO APPEAR THIS MORNING HAS NOT BEEN PROVIDED TO THE OPPOSING PARTY. UPON CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE IS GRANTED; IT SHALL PAY ALL TRANSFER FEES. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 - SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THIS DATE. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF OCT-18-2023 CONTINUED TO DEC-20-2023 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR DEFENDANT TO COMPLETE TRANSFER. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
CHANGE OF VENUE FEE FILED BY DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA, A CALIFORNIA CORPORAITON HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR FEB-27-2024 CONTINUED TO APR-30-2024 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 TO: DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL FOR FAILURE TO COMPLETE TRANSFER. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-19-2023 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Jul-19-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendant HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA's MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Daniel Nickfardjam, Esq., Quill & Arrow, LLP, (661) 762-4091, for Plaintiff.

No other appearances in this matter,

Ruling - Argued. The court confirms that notice to appear this morning was not provided to the opposing party. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Hyundai Motor America's unopposed motion to transfer venue is granted; it shall pay all transfer fees.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)
